headline: Let him cook: How Weber Blackstone CEO Roger Dahle went from upstart to the biggest name in grilling
summary: The Decoder podcast features Weber Blackstone CEO Roger Dahle in its Fourth of July grilling episode. Dahle discusses his journey leading the prominent outdoor cooking company. The episode explores how grilling businesses operate and succeed.

headline: Rocket Lab is buying Iridium’s satellite network for $8 billion to take on SpaceX
summary: Rocket Lab announced an $8 billion acquisition of Iridium Communications. This deal merges Rocket Lab’s launch services with Iridium’s satellite communications network. The acquisition positions Rocket Lab to directly challenge SpaceX in the space industry.

headline: The Flipper Zero creators’ Busy Bar productivity display will go on sale next month
summary: The Flipper Zero team is launching the Busy Bar, a new productivity multitool. The device, first announced in April 2025, will be available starting July 14th. It features a pixelated LED display designed to boost user productivity.

headline: How to end a TV show
summary: Concluding a TV series is challenging, especially for mystery-packed shows. Many shows, like Lost, struggle to deliver satisfying resolutions after building complex narratives. The article explores why few series manage to provide a truly effective ending.

headline: Nex Playground Review
summary: This article provides a comprehensive review of the Nex Playground. It likely covers the device's features, performance, and user experience. Expect insights into its suitability for interactive gaming and entertainment.

headline: 'It Is Not Realistic for Us to Absorb All Component Cost Increases' — Sony Says It Won't Sell Hardware at a 'Significant' Loss as Concern Grows Over PS6 Price
summary: Sony stated it will not sell hardware at a 'significant' loss, addressing growing concerns about the PS6 price. The company cites rising component costs, dubbed the 'RAMpocalypse', as a major factor. This stance suggests a potential higher price point for the upcoming PlayStation 6 console.

headline: Nintendo Drops the Price of Switch 2 Memory Cards for Its Summer Sale
summary: Nintendo is selling officially licensed Switch 2 memory cards for $39 during its 2026 Summer Sale. This limited-time offer provides the cheapest way to instantly double the console's storage. Gamers can expand their Switch 2 capacity at a significantly reduced price.

headline: Japanese Theme Park Gets Escape Room Experience in Which Participants Wear a 'Bomb Collar' That Gives a Real Electric Shock
summary: A Japanese theme park is introducing an escape room that features a controversial 'bomb collar'. Participants will wear the device, which delivers real electric shocks. This extreme element is designed to intensify the game's immersive experience.

headline: Henry Zeffman: Andy Burnham offers a blueprint for his premiership
summary: Andy Burnham presented a vision for what his time as prime minister would entail. Political analyst Henry Zeffman reports that Burnham offered a general sense but few specific details. The speech outlined a broad blueprint rather than a detailed policy agenda.

headline: Mum rescued from Venezuela rubble with newborn baby tells BBC how he helped her survive
summary: A mother and her 18-day-old baby, Juan David, were miraculously rescued from rubble in Venezuela. The baby's survival has become a symbol of hope following devastating earthquakes. The mother shared her harrowing survival story, crediting her newborn for her strength.

headline: The Good Life actress Dame Penelope Keith dies aged 86
summary: Dame Penelope Keith, renowned actress from 'The Good Life,' has passed away at 86. Tributes are pouring in for the 'comic genius' celebrated for her iconic roles. She starred in some of Britain's most beloved sitcoms.

headline: Five dead in shooting at youth welfare centre in northern Germany
summary: A shooting incident at a youth welfare center in northern Germany resulted in five fatalities. Police have made two arrests in connection with the attack. One of the arrested individuals has been identified as a suspect in the ongoing investigation.
